heroTitle1: Turn a GitHub repo into an
heroDesc: One URL visualizes file structure, function call flow, and DB relationships. Structural warning detection included — all free.
urlHint: Doesn't have to be your own repo — any public repo works.
urlHintLoginSuffix: Sign in with GitHub to start analysis automatically.

steps.title: Paste a GitHub repo URL
steps.desc: Enter a public repo URL, or a connected private one.

steps.title: Automatic analysis — ~10–60s
steps.desc: A tree-sitter AST-based static analysis engine extracts file, function, and DB relationships across 13 languages including Java, TypeScript, Python, and Go.

steps.title: Get an interactive diagram
steps.desc: Click nodes to trace flow, review warnings, and share with teammates.

features.title: File & Function Structure View
features.desc: Renders import relationships and function call flow as a clear diagram.

features.title: End-to-End Flow Tracing
features.desc: One click on an API endpoint auto-traces the call flow. See where it starts and where it ends in the sidebar, and replay the flow.

features.title: Structural Warning Detection
features.desc: Statically detects 15 risky patterns ahead of time — circular dependencies, DDD boundary violations, DB layer bypasses, and more.

features.title: Live Collaboration & Community
features.desc: Invite teammates with an invite code and co-edit with cursor overlays. Publish graphs to the community for architecture review.

features.title: Image & AI Context Export
features.desc: Save the full graph as a full-resolution PNG, or export function-name-and-comment style Markdown to use directly as AI prompt context.

featuredReposDesc: We analyze popular open source projects every day — click to jump straight to the results

free.name: Free
features: Graph version history · export warnings as Markdown
Collaboration sessions up to 6 people (incl. owner)

pro.name: Pro · Desktop
features: Choose 1 personal seat or N team seats
🖥 Desktop app (local folder analysis) coming soon
pro.note: Individual billing is temporarily paused while we build recurring subscriptions. Team billing is still available on the Teams page.
